samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining EW green time is greater than 10 clocks, force it to be 10 clocks. If the remaining EW green time is less than 10 clocks, do not change the remaining time. The EW and NS lights are paired. When the EW light is green, the NS light is red. When the EW light is red, the NS light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>State transition logic.</description>
            <switch>
                <case id="idle">Transition to s1_red.</case>
                <case id="s1_red">Transition to s3_green after 5 clock cycles.</case>
                <case id="s2_yellow">Transition to s1_red after 5 clock cycles.</case>
                <case id="s3_green">Transition to s2_yellow after 5 clock cycles.</case>
            </switch>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <description>Counter logic.</description>
            <switch>
                <case id="idle">Set to 10.</case>
                <case id="s1_red">Set to 10 if pass_request is active and green is high. Otherwise, decrement by 1.</case>
                <case id="s2_yellow">Set to 60 if green is low and p_green is high. Otherwise, decrement by 1.</case>
                <case id="s3_green">Set to 5 if red is low and p_red is high. Otherwise, decrement by 1.</case>
            </switch>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>Reset state variables and counters. Handle state transitions based on input signals. Update output signals based on counter values.</description>
        <switch>
            <case id="reset">Set state to idle, red to 0, yellow to 0, green to 0, and cnt to 0.</case>
            <case id="state_transition">If pass_request is active and green is high, set cnt to 10. Otherwise, if green is high, set cnt to 60. Otherwise, if yellow is high, set cnt to 5. Otherwise, if red is high, set cnt to 10.</case>
            <case id="output_assignment">Set red to p_red, yellow to p_yellow, and green to p_green.</case>
        </switch>
    </implementation>
</module>
